evals.prompt: Audit the current HSC agent codebase for FACT/JACIE chain of custody compliance gaps. Tell me which audit events are missing and what an inspector would flag.
evals.expected_output: A gap analysis listing the 10 missing events (NMDP_DOCUMENT_RECEIVED, NMDP_DOCUMENT_EXTRACTED, NMDP_DOCUMENT_CONFIRMED, PRODUCT_CONDITION_CHECKED, RESULTS_VALIDATED, RELEASE_CRITERIA_CHECKED, ADMINISTRATION_STARTED, ADMINISTRATION_COMPLETED, ADVERSE_EVENT, PRODUCT_DISPOSED), the PHYSICAL_INSPECTION_FAILED schema mismatch, and incomplete details payloads.
assertions: Identifies at least 8 of the 10 missing events by name
Flags PHYSICAL_INSPECTION_FAILED as emitted but not in CHECK constraint
Notes that receipt phase lacks PRODUCT_CONDITION_CHECKED for condition-on-receipt
Notes that administration phase lacks ADMINISTRATION_STARTED and ADMINISTRATION_COMPLETED
Mentions ADVERSE_EVENT as mandatory for adverse reaction reporting
References FACT/JACIE standards or FDA 21 CFR 1271
Mentions two-person verification requirement for release and bedside
Identifies incomplete details payloads as a gap

evals.prompt: Create the Alembic migration to add the 10 missing FACT/JACIE audit events to the CHECK constraint in audit.audit_events.
evals.expected_output: A complete Alembic migration file that drops the existing CHECK constraint and creates a new one with all 34 action_types (24 existing + 10 new).
assertions: Uses ALTER TABLE audit.audit_events DROP CONSTRAINT and ADD CONSTRAINT pattern
Includes all 24 existing action_types (no accidental removals)

Adds all 10 new events: NMDP_DOCUMENT_RECEIVED, NMDP_DOCUMENT_EXTRACTED, NMDP_DOCUMENT_CONFIRMED, PRODUCT_CONDITION_CHECKED, RESULTS_VALIDATED, RELEASE_CRITERIA_CHECKED, ADMINISTRATION_STARTED, ADMINISTRATION_COMPLETED, ADVERSE_EVENT, PRODUCT_DISPOSED
Includes a downgrade function that restores the original constraint
Has a descriptive docstring mentioning FACT/JACIE compliance
Uses op.execute() with raw SQL (not op.alter_column)
File follows the project's migration naming convention (sequential number prefix)

evals.prompt: Add the ADMINISTRATION_STARTED and ADMINISTRATION_COMPLETED emit_event calls to the appropriate node, with the correct details payload for FACT/JACIE compliance.
evals.expected_output: Code changes that emit both events with the required details fields (administered_by, start_time, route, location for STARTED; end_time, volume_infused_ml, complications for COMPLETED).
assertions: Emits ADMINISTRATION_STARTED with resource_type='din' and outcome='success'
ADMINISTRATION_STARTED details include administered_by, start_time, route, location
Emits ADMINISTRATION_COMPLETED with resource_type='din' and outcome='success'
ADMINISTRATION_COMPLETED details include administered_by, end_time, volume_infused_ml, complications
Both events use the DIN as resource_id
actor field is set to the administering person (not 'system')
Events are emitted in the correct node (EIS or a new administration node)
Includes or references corresponding test cases

evals.prompt: Fix the PHYSICAL_INSPECTION_FAILED bug — it's emitted in results.py but not in the CHECK constraint. Replace it with the FACT/JACIE-compliant event.
evals.expected_output: Code change in results.py replacing PHYSICAL_INSPECTION_FAILED with PRODUCT_CONDITION_CHECKED (outcome=failure), enriched with the required details fields.
assertions: Replaces action_type from PHYSICAL_INSPECTION_FAILED to PRODUCT_CONDITION_CHECKED
Sets outcome to 'failure' (not 'success')
Includes temperature_celsius in details
Includes packaging_intact (bool) in details
Includes accepted=False in details
Includes rejection_reason in details
Updates or adds corresponding test case
Does NOT add PHYSICAL_INSPECTION_FAILED to the CHECK constraint (uses standard naming)

evals.prompt: Explain what a FACT/JACIE inspector checks during an accreditation visit regarding the electronic audit trail. What would they ask to see and what are the most common deficiencies?
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive explanation covering traceability walk-through, concurrent recording, two-person verification, deviation management, chain integrity, and record retention, with the top 5 common deficiencies.
assertions: Explains the traceability walk-through (inspector picks a random DIN and traces the full lifecycle)
Mentions concurrent recording requirement (timestamps must be real-time, not backdated)
Explains two-person verification for release and bedside verification
Covers deviation documentation requirement (every escalation must have resolution)
Mentions chain integrity verification (SHA-256 hash chain)
States 10-year retention requirement per FDA 21 CFR 1271.55
Lists at least 3 of the top 5 common deficiencies (incomplete traceability, missing signatures, undocumented deviations)
References specific action_types that satisfy each requirement
